source.notes: Raw sheet row 1 contains headers. Metadata/sample-rate rows are skipped before processing frame data.

uncertain.note: Dominant shoulder horizontal angle requires side/sign convention confirmation.
Uses absolute dominant-shoulder rotation because external/internal sign convention is not defined in the workbook.
Uses provisional max-ER marker because event tags are not present.
Human confirmation needed for sign convention and event window.
Uses magnitude because knee flexion/extension sign convention is not confirmed.
Dominant shoulder rotation velocity is used as the shoulder IR velocity proxy.

eventMarkers.note: Chart markers show measured series peaks only. Confirmed event frame tags were not found in the workbook.

metrics.name: Shoulder Horizontal Abduction
metrics.finding: Peak dominant-shoulder horizontal angle is 111.5° in the processed capture.
metrics.whyItMatters: Shoulder horizontal position helps describe arm path and trunk relationship during the throw.
metrics.trainingFocus: Review with confirmed foot-plant and arm-cocking frames before grading.
metrics.reviewNote: Dominant shoulder horizontal angle requires side/sign convention confirmation.

metrics.name: Shoulder External Rotation
metrics.finding: Dominant-shoulder rotation reaches 161.5°; direction requires lab confirmation.
metrics.whyItMatters: External rotation is a key loading marker before shoulder internal rotation acceleration.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm the rotation sign convention and max-ER event before assigning a performance grade.
metrics.reviewNote: Uses absolute dominant-shoulder rotation because external/internal sign convention is not defined in the workbook.

metrics.name: Elbow Flexion Angle
metrics.finding: Elbow flexion is 114.6° at the provisional max-ER marker.
metrics.whyItMatters: Elbow angle helps contextualize arm timing into acceleration.
metrics.trainingFocus: Validate against tagged max-ER and ball-release frames.
metrics.reviewNote: Uses provisional max-ER marker because event tags are not present.

metrics.name: Hip-Shoulder Separation
metrics.finding: Peak trunk separation in the workbook is -35.8°.
metrics.whyItMatters: Separation is a proximal-to-distal transfer marker for the throwing sequence.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm sign convention and preferred event window before grading.
metrics.reviewNote: Human confirmation needed for sign convention and event window.

metrics.name: Lead Knee Extension Velocity
metrics.whyItMatters: Lead-knee speed helps describe how the front side transfers force into the trunk and arm.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm whether positive direction represents extension before using as a block grade.
metrics.reviewNote: Uses magnitude because knee flexion/extension sign convention is not confirmed.

metrics.name: Shoulder Internal Rotation Velocity
metrics.whyItMatters: Shoulder IR velocity is the final high-speed transfer marker before release.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm internal-rotation direction and ball-release timing.
metrics.reviewNote: Dominant shoulder rotation velocity is used as the shoulder IR velocity proxy.
